steps.title: Use Text 1's skepticism
steps.content: Text 2 presents Silva and colleagues' method as a possible improvement in automated genre classification. Text 1, however, questions whether genre classification is useful in the first place, calling genre categories ambiguous, subjective, and simplistic.

steps.title: Reject technical praise
steps.content: Choice A would treat better classification as a promising advance, but Text 1 would be less interested in that technical improvement because it doubts the value of genre categories. Choice C introduces listener enjoyment, which is not the point of disagreement in either text. Choice D focuses on whether some genres are easier for systems to recognize, while Text 1's objection is broader. The response most consistent with Text 1 is that genre may not be the best framework for understanding music. The answer is B.
